review_notes: Row 1 (Ткаченко Ірина) has 'TOUR-LEADER' in the order column (col 7) and no passport number — likely the group leader (role 18); confirm role mapping.
Canonical fields sesso, comune_nascita, provincia_nascita, stato_nascita, cittadinanza, luogo_rilascio are entirely absent from this list — must be collected or defaulted elsewhere.

Name order is ambiguous: some names appear as 'SURNAME FIRSTNAME' (all-caps) and some as 'Firstname Surname' mixed-case (e.g. 'Ткаченко Ірина', 'Lebedynska Marharyta') — manual review needed to confirm surname/first split for each row.
Column 6 ('Номер') contains room assignments (e.g. 'SGL 1', 'TWIN 1') which are not a canonical field but may be useful for grouping; ignored in mapping.
Column 7 header contains obfuscated text ('№**** ****замовлення') suggesting a booking/order number — not a canonical field, ignored.
Column 8 ('Харчув.') is a meal-plan code (e.g. 'BB') — not a canonical field, ignored.
Column 9 ('VIP') is always empty in the sample — ignored.
Children rows (e.g. rows with birth years 2014, 2018, 2017) share the same order number as an adult — confirm whether they should be mapped as 'membro gruppo' (20) or a child role.
Row 1 (Ткаченко Ірина) has no passport number — if role 18 (capogruppo) requires a document number, this must be resolved manually.
Date format in source is DD.MM.YYYY; dotted_date normalizer converts dots to slashes to yield DD/MM/YYYY — confirm this matches the Alloggiati Web expected format.

Row 1 (№1, Ткаченко Ірина) has 'TOUR-LEADER' in column 7 (order number field) instead of a booking number — this person is the group leader and should be coded '18' (capogruppo); passport/document field is also empty for her.
Column 7 header is '№**** ****замовлення' (order/booking number) but doubles as a role marker for the tour leader row; humans should confirm role mapping logic.
sesso (gender) is not present in the list — must be collected or inferred externally.
comune_nascita, provincia_nascita, stato_nascita, cittadinanza, luogo_rilascio are all absent from the list — required Alloggiati fields that must be supplied from another source or entered manually.
Some guests share the same booking number in column 7 (e.g. rows 2–3 share '66836361618'), suggesting room-sharing pairs — verify room assignment logic in column 6.
Column 6 ('Номер') contains room type/number but is empty for the second person in each pair; this is a display-merge pattern, not a data gap — confirm pairing logic.
Name casing is mixed (some ALL-CAPS, some Title Case, some Cyrillic) — normalisation of name_order 'surname_first' may need manual verification for Cyrillic-script names.
Row 1 name 'Ткаченко Ірина' is in Cyrillic; Alloggiati typically requires Latin-script transliteration — human review needed.
Passport numbers beginning with 'G' (e.g. GU900515, GE344509) may be child passports or internal documents — verify document type is indeed 'passport' for all entries in column 5.

Several rows (3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15, 17, 19) appear to be second guests sharing a room with the preceding row; their room number cell (col 6) is blank — confirm they are treated as 'membro gruppo' (20) not as separate single guests.
Column 9 ('VIP') is uniformly empty — no action required, but confirm it carries no codable meaning.
Column 8 ('Харчув.' / meal plan) is uniformly 'BB' or empty — not a canonical Alloggiati field, no mapping applied.

None of the canonical fields (sesso, data_nascita, comune_nascita, provincia_nascita, stato_nascita, cittadinanza, tipo_documento, numero_documento, luogo_rilascio) are present in this list — the table contains only hotel/logistical data and guest names.
Row 0 appears to be a summary/totals row (values like 15, 0, 13, 41, 28, 41 in columns 8–15) rather than a data row; it is covered by header_rows=2 skip but should be verified.
Column 20 ('Parcheggio') contains parking notes (e.g. '1 auto', '1 furgone con carrello') — not a canonical field but may be useful for voucher generation.
Column 21 ('Varie per voucher') contains miscellaneous notes (e.g. 'CELIACA') — dietary/special requirements should be reviewed manually.
Second name slot (columns 18–19) is only populated for TWIN rooms with 2 PAX; rows with a single guest will have empty columns 18–19 — ensure downstream processing handles this gracefully.

Column 0 ('Gruppo') identifies the functional group (e.g. 'Area tecnica arrivi', 'Quartiertappa 1', 'Radioinformazioni arrivi') — this may imply different roles but no explicit role column exists; all mapped as default_role '20' (membro gruppo).
No document data is present; Alloggiati Web submission will require manual supplementation of all identity fields.
Column 7 ('Tratt.') value 'HBB' likely means half-board with breakfast — not a canonical field but relevant for vouchers.
Hotel category (column 3) is '3*' for all rows — no canonical mapping but useful context.
Check-in date (column 12) and check-out date (column 13) are present but not in the canonical field list; dotted_date normalizer not applicable here as dates are already in dd/mm/yyyy slash format.

None of the canonical fields (sesso, data_nascita, comune_nascita, provincia_nascita, stato_nascita, cittadinanza, tipo_documento, numero_documento, luogo_rilascio) are present in this rooming list — the list contains only group/hotel logistics data.
Row 0 appears to be a summary/totals row (contains counts 15, 0, 13, 41, 28, 41 in columns 8–15) and should be skipped as a header row along with row 1.
Column 18 (Cognome 2) and column 19 (Nome 2) are only populated for twin/double rooms sharing two guests; second name slot should only be used when column 18 is non-empty.

Column 0 (Gruppo) contains group/team names (e.g. 'Area tecnica arrivi', 'Quartiertappa 1', 'Radioinformazioni arrivi') which may map to different Alloggiati roles — all are currently defaulted to '20' (membro gruppo) but a human should verify if any group deserves role '16' (ospite singolo) or another code.
Column 7 (Tratt./board type: HBB) is not a canonical field but may be needed for voucher generation.
Column 20 (Parcheggio) contains parking notes (e.g. '1 auto', '1 furgone con carrello') — not a canonical field but may be relevant operationally.
Column 21 (Varie per voucher) contains special notes (e.g. 'CELIACA' for row 18) — not a canonical field but should be reviewed for guest special requirements.
Check-in date is 04/06/2026 and check-out is 05/06/2026 for all rows — verify these are correct and not placeholder dates.

No document or personal identity data is present; Alloggiati Web submission will require manual entry of sesso, data_nascita, comune_nascita, provincia_nascita, stato_nascita, cittadinanza, tipo_documento, numero_documento, luogo_rilascio for every guest.
Second name slot should be suppressed when column 18 is empty to avoid creating phantom guest records.

Row index 2 (No.=1): Last Name cell is 'GUIDE NOWAK' — this person appears to be a tour guide, not a regular guest. The word 'GUIDE' is a role marker embedded in the surname cell and must be stripped before parsing the actual surname 'NOWAK'. Role should likely be '19' (capogruppo/guide) rather than '20'.

Row index 3 (No.=2): Last Name cell is 'Ks. KOWALCZYK' — 'Ks.' is a Polish honorific/title (Ksiądz = Catholic priest) embedded in the surname cell and must be stripped before parsing surname 'KOWALCZYK'.

Fields entirely absent from this list: sesso, comune_nascita, provincia_nascita, stato_nascita, cittadinanza, tipo_documento, numero_documento, luogo_rilascio. All mandatory Alloggiati fields beyond date-of-birth must be collected from another source or entered manually.

ROOMING LIST column (index 4) contains room-type codes (SGL, DBL, TWN) — these are accommodation/pairing indicators, not Alloggiati canonical fields, so they are not mapped but may be useful for grouping guests into rooms.
No gender (sesso) column is present; sex must be determined manually or inferred from first names.
No document information (tipo_documento, numero_documento, luogo_rilascio) is present anywhere in the list.
No nationality/citizenship (cittadinanza, stato_nascita) information is present.
No place of birth (comune_nascita, provincia_nascita) information is present.
date_of_birth values appear in DD/MM/YYYY format and are passed through as-is; verify they match Alloggiati expected format.
DBL/TWN rows appear in pairs sharing the same surname — pairing logic for married couples or roommates should be verified manually.
The 'default_role' is set to '20' (membro gruppo) assuming this is a group list; if any individuals are solo travellers the role may need to be '16' (ospite singolo) — human review required.
No explicit capogruppo ('18') marker column exists; the guide in row 1 ('GUIDE NOWAK') is the most likely candidate for role '18' or '19', but this cannot be determined automatically.

Row index 2 (No.=1): Last Name is 'GUIDE NOWAK' — contains role prefix 'GUIDE'; this person is likely a tour guide (Alloggiati role 'CA' or similar), not a standard group member. Human must verify and reassign role.
Row index 3 (No.=2): Last Name is 'Ks. KOWALCZYK' — 'Ks.' is a Polish honorific for a Catholic priest; the actual surname is KOWALCZYK. Human must strip prefix before submission.

Fields entirely absent from this list: sesso, comune_nascita, provincia_nascita, stato_nascita, cittadinanza, tipo_documento, numero_documento, luogo_rilascio — all mandatory for Alloggiati Web; must be collected separately.
ROOMING LIST column (index 4) contains room-type codes (SGL, DBL, TWN) — useful for pairing guests into shared rooms but not a canonical Alloggiati field; ignored in mapping.

Rows 3–4 (Mazur/Mazur), 5–6 (Kowalski/Kowalska), 7–8 (WÓJCIK/WÓJCIK), 9–10 (Wiśniewski/Wiśniewska), 11–12 (Krawczyk/Krawczyk), 13–14 (Kaczmarek/Kaczmarek), 17–18 (Lis-Dąbrowska/Dąbrowski) appear to be couples sharing a DBL room — human should verify whether names match.
Rows 15–16 (Sikora/Zając) share a TWN room but have different surnames — may be non-related guests; human should verify.
No 'sesso' (gender) data present; cannot be inferred from names reliably — must be supplied manually.

Row with No.=1 (GUIDE NOWAK, SGL room): if this person is the tour guide, Alloggiati role should be '17' (accompagnatore) rather than '20' (membro gruppo) — requires human decision.
Row with No.=2 (Ks. KOWALCZYK): role unclear — could be a standard group member or a special role; human must confirm.

Row 0 (Klimowicz Ks. Tomasz): firstname cell contains honorific prefix 'Ks.' (Polish abbreviation for 'Ksiądz', i.e. Catholic priest) — this is a title, not part of the given name; a human should strip 'Ks.' before submission.

tipo_documento is derived from the document-number column (col 4) via doc_type_passport normalizer; the actual document type (passport, ID card, etc.) is not explicitly stated and should be verified.

Document numbers in col 4 vary in format (e.g. 'GK 7261054', 'MZ 5093412', 'RW 6082735' have two-letter prefixes; others have three-letter prefixes) — format inconsistency should be reviewed to confirm all are the same document type.
Row 18 (Bienias Aleksandra, DOB 18/04/2013): guest appears to be a minor (approx. 11 years old) — role may need to be adjusted and accompanying adult identified.

No group/family structure is encoded in the list; rows sharing a surname (e.g. multiple Gawron, Lasocka, Jastrzębska, Grabowska) may be family members requiring role differentiation (capofamiglia vs. membro).
default_role set to '16' (ospite singolo) as no role column or group marker is present; a human should confirm whether any rows should be '17' (capofamiglia) or '20' (membro gruppo).

Row 0 (Klimowicz, Ks. Tomasz): firstname cell contains honorific prefix 'Ks.' (Polish abbreviation for 'Ksiądz' = Catholic priest). This may indicate a clergy role or group leader — human should confirm whether role should differ from default and how to handle the prefix in the name.

Row 18 (Bienias, Aleksandra, DOB 18/04/2013): date of birth indicates a minor (child, ~11 years old). Role may need to be adjusted (e.g. '19' for bambino/child under Alloggiati rules) — human must verify.
